name: Đồi cát Du lịch Hòa Thắng

reviews.author: Peter Sittsam
reviews.text: went there by myself by motorbike, nice place to walk around barefoot and enjoy some dunes, and I think that´s the best way to do because like that you can somehow try to avoid the big tourist groups with their ATM´s and jeeps driving around everywhere und you can explore a few different spots nearby yourself. It´s one of the very few places in south east asia where you get to see actual sand dunes.

reviews.author: Jolie Li
reviews.text: When you google “bãi cát trắng Mũi Né” (white sand dunes Mui Ne), it actually shows “red sand dunes”, which is a completely different place! The red dunes don’t have ATVs for rent, and you’ll have to walk uphill on hot sand — so if you’re reading my review, you’ve found the right white sand dunes. Note that right next to this spot, there are 2 more place on this street next to each other, i passed by them when i got out, it seemed they also offer ATV and Jeep and all that, u can check them out as well, maybe their price is cheaper Entrance fee: very affordable — only 15,000 VND per adult. ATV rental: quite pricey for Vietnam, around 800,000 to 1,000,000 VND for 20 minutes, depending on the type of ATV. Also, you can’t drive it alone; a staff member must ride with you (similar to how jet skis work in Vietnam). Personally, I didn’t like that part. They also offer sand sliding for 50,000 VND per slide, which I think is more reasonable, and you can rent jeeps here too. Overall: It’s worth visiting if you’re already in the area — especially for kids to experience something fun and unique — but I wouldn’t make a long trip just for this spot.

reviews.author: James Bond
reviews.text: Don’t go there… it’s a tourist trap! Illusion of desert ? Overused , polluted, dirty , surrounded by windmills ! It’s a beautiful area destroyed by people on jeeps, quads who are not able to walk 10 minutes … and respect the nature

reviews.author: Tobias Baumgartner
reviews.text: There are jeeps and ATVs everywhere, making a lot of noise. The place itself is beautiful..♥️ but the heavy tourism is really ruining it. I walked everything on foot, and it’s only about 10 minutes from hill to hill .. you don’t need a vehicle here at all. Everywhere the vehicles pass, there’s trash on the ground. Such a shame.

reviews.author: Mariusz Redwanz
reviews.text: We booked the jeep tour through our hotel, but honestly it doesn’t matter where you book it—hotels usually just call the same operators and arrange the pickup. Important tip about the “sunset tour”: it’s often run in reverse order, starts around 13:30, lasts about 4 hours, and you can end up back before sunset. Our hotel also said "morning weather is more reliable" because evenings are often cloudy and the sun may not be visible—and what we experienced confirmed that. What happened on the sunrise tour (and what to watch out for) Pickup was at 4:30 AM. The driver didn’t speak English. After ~30 minutes we stopped at an almost empty parking area (toilet available). Then we were taken to someone who demanded 600,000 VND to take us by **quad/ATV** to the sand dunes. They claimed it was “4 km” and said we wouldn’t make sunrise on foot—this felt misleading. We paid, and the two of us were put on one ATV and dropped on basically the first higher dune. Only later we understood that when the hotel said something like "20 minutes for 300k with driver, 800k without driver", they were actually referring to this ATV transfer, not some optional extra ride. The hotel suggested negotiating—so be ready to do that if you want. ATVs arrive one after another, so it quickly becomes crowded. At one point when we tried walking to another dune, staff started shouting, but when we asked why we couldn’t go, they eventually gestured that we *can* go. Also: it’s not a “real desert”—more like a few dunes, and you can see trees/buildings in many directions. Still, the sunrise was really nice. Practical tips * It was a bit cool, but no need for a hoodie/jacket. * Bring something small to sit on, because you may wait 30–40 minutes for sunrise. * Flip-flops/sandals are ideal—you’ll take your shoes off a lot in the sand. After sunrise We went back to the dune we started on and were immediately put on an ATV to another spot—this ride was much more “exciting” (a real adrenaline bump). They take photos of everyone on the ATV near a small lake, and then you can walk to dunes that are more photogenic than the first ones. Other stops * Red Sand Dunes: you explore on your own—walk up nearby dunes, take photos. * Fishing Village: similar, short stop. * Fairy Stream: you walk it yourself. You can walk instead of paying for the ATV (or at least try to negotiate). You can also rent a scooter and do everything independently. That said, the jeep tour itself was reasonably priced and we enjoyed it—and we don’t really regret paying for the ATV either.

reviews.author: Dmitrii Burmistrov
reviews.text: Avoid this tourist trap! We went to the White Sand Dunes with a Jeep tour. The driver asked if we wanted to rent ATVs (800k for 20 minutes), and we agreed after seeing new, powerful quads at the entrance. He confirmed we’d use those good ones, so we agreed and were told to pay upfront. Our driver, who actually worked for the ATV company, told the ticket seller to let us ride first and pay later, claiming he would “lend” us money But here’s the catch: They took us up the hill on good quads, disappeared for 5 minutes, and returned with completely different, old, and damaged ATVs (see the attached photo). These were smaller, less powerful, and frankly dangerous-looking. Feeling cheated, we decided not to ride at all. Then it got worse: We asked how to return, and they said it was free. They took us down by ATV, only to demand 300,000 VND per person afterward—for a mere 2-minute ride! This charge was never mentioned earlier. It’s literally just a 15-20 minute walk you can easily do yourself (if it’s in the morning) Luckily, we argued strongly enough that they finally let us go, mostly because our driver had “approved” our delayed payment arrangement. So - Don’t rent ATVs here; it’s a scam. - Visit independently and just walk up — it’s easy and free. - Red Sand Dunes were way better anyway. Stay safe and save your money!

reviews.author: allPhoto Paul
reviews.text: It is generally nice to see. Nothing super spectacular. Annoying prices of the companies selling you car or quad rides. It's not so massive so u can easily walk around

reviews.author: Wade Wilson
reviews.text: White Sand Dunes. The name really nails it: they’re dunes. And they’re white. Slow clap for whoever came up with that marketing gem. What’s waiting for you? Sand. Miles of it. A sun so aggressive it makes your sunscreen cry. And, if you’re really into pain, overpriced ATVs you can rent to tear around like a knockoff Mad Max extra while a local watches his suspension die in real time. But here’s the kicker: you get to pay for this privilege. Fifteen thousand dong to enter. Ten thousand dong to park. Yes, dong is the currency. Stop giggling, you filthy, filthy humans, I know exactly what’s running through your minds. Every tourist reads the price list and suddenly reverts to middle school. Meanwhile, the locals are taking your dongs with the biggest poker face ever, thinking, “Yup, another foreigner paying real money to sweat in a sandbox.” So, congratulations. You’ve just shelled out 25,000 dong for the chance to roast alive while sand gets into places your doctor doesn’t want to hear about. Sure, the dunes are kinda pretty if you squint through the sweat dripping into your eyes. But after five minutes you’ll realize you could’ve spent the same cash on iced coffee and actually enjoyed being alive. It’s basically Tatooine. But without lightsabers, without the Force, and without a young Harrison Ford to distract you. Just you, the sand, and the crushing realization that you paid for it.

reviews.author: Salman Alfarisi
reviews.text: I recently visited the White Sand Dunes and had a pretty good experience overall. I loved the dunes, but I went during the rainy season, so the sky was a bit unpredictable. Still, it was quite a sight! The place is easy to access, and there are lots of locals offering sand sliding for just 3000 VND – you can slide to your heart's content. I also took an ATV ride, and the driver had great skills, taking me to every corner and up some steep dunes. It was quite the adventure! However, I do think it would be even better to visit during the sunny season or summer for clearer skies and better views. There was no traffic during my visit, which was a plus. The guides were friendly and helped me take some photos, though their photography skills were just okay. They offer tours starting at 4:30 AM or 1:00 PM, so you have options depending on what time of day you'd like to explore. Overall, it was a fun experience despite the weather!

reviews.author: suraj singh
reviews.text: The White Sand Dunes in Mui Ne, while a bit farther from the town itself, are definitely worth the visit. This vast expanse of white sand desert offers a thrilling adventure, especially with the larger dunes that provide plenty of space for off-road activities. You have two main options for exploring the dunes: hiring a private ATV or opting for a jeep ride. Walking across the vast area is not recommended, as the sand makes it tiring and the distance is considerable. We decided to hire an ATV for about 800,000 VNĐ for a 20-minute ride, which seemed a bit pricey but was totally worth it for the fun and excitement it offered. We visited around sunset, which added a magical touch to the experience. The highlight was being able to drive the ATV ourselves, with a local guide accompanying us to ensure safety. The real adrenaline rush came when the guide took over the controls, speeding through the dunes and navigating different terrains with skill. Overall, it's a thrilling and memorable experience that’s highly recommended for anyone visiting Mui Ne.

reviews.author: Hafizun Mushtafa
reviews.text: A huge white sand dunes and a like with a windmill view. The view is pretty. You can see the sea from the top of the dunes. The beauty of the view here is heavily depend on weather and sunshine. Most people will come during sunrise. There is a jeep service provided to go around the sand dunes and will stop in a few checkpoints. The price is 800000 VND per jeep. For me it's quite expensive but if you came with 4 people it can be okay. Plus the jeep will not bring you to the place that you want. Another alternative is that you can take an ATV which also costs 800000 but has a time limit of 20 minutes which I think is ridiculous. Or else you can just walk from the starting point to the huge sand dunes. Probably will take a longer time if you want to save money but if your time is limited, a jeep is the best way to explore here. Overall I think it's a good place to visit at Muine but if you hesitate to go or to save budget simply don't go as some might feel it is quite a waste.

reviews.author: Mai Nguyen
reviews.text: Awesome place! You have experience of being in a little desert. Great views from the top of the hills. Just expect to get a lot of sand blown to you as it’s quite windy. Should try the ATV ride which is pretty cool and thrilling. The sand-sliding which costs 50k vnd for unlimited ride is also recommended. I could do only one slide because it’s very tiring to climb back up. Highly recommended!

reviews.author: Q Xan
reviews.text: Huge area of sand Dunes, although the end is not white. The Dunes are huge and there is a lake. The atv or car (with driver) costs 800 000 VND. Some people also walk but it's hard because it's a long way and not easy. On the biggest dune you can slide down on a plastic sheet or drive down your atv or car. The cars do some exciting runs if you want, so it's worth the money. If you want it less exciting, the driver just brings you to the beautiful stops and you get enough time for taking pictures and enjoying the breathtaking scenery. You should go on the early morning or for sunset, during the day (between 10 and 17) it's too hot

reviews.author: Emily Carter
reviews.text: Great experience here. As we are backpacking we were hesitant to pay for the ATV but the woman let us get one for 600,000 between two people worked out okay. It was definitely worth it, stopped for sunrise then continued on the ATV down the huge sand dunes! Very thrilling experience and 100% worth the money! Landscape is beautiful and worth seeing, makes the red sand dunes look like nothing!

reviews.author: cameron holmes
reviews.text: Beautiful place, so worth coming. Sunrise is awesome. You can’t see much at sunrise as it’s so dark, but when the sun comes over the horizon it’s a spectacular sight. I believe that it is worth paying the quad bike. I think it’s 600k pp but try and barter them. They took us on a rollercoaster of a ride around the dunes! Definitely woke me up XD. There are some spectacular views of dunes, a lake and wind turbines. What an epic start to a day.

reviews.author: Sheila A.
reviews.text: TOURIST TRAP I think most people who recommend coming here are tourists who only care about taking pictures. Unfortunately, this is also enables locals who are only looking for cash grabs. 300k for one person for a quad which we decided not to do as it was literally the price per person for our entire 4-hour jeep tour. Heard from two groups of tourists that they drive you up the dune at a dangerous speed, knowing they have passengers behind. You have a minute or two to take pictures (not forgetting other quads are also driving by erratically) and they drive you right back down. At the cafe by the entrance, you see locals who operate this (probably unregulated) quad “tour” are just gathered together around a dude in a hammock who was counting money — probably the head honcho. The entrance has some sad circus design that has not rhyme or reason with the dune. There were dogs everywhere who was eating off scraps from the cantine and drinking water from a bucket close to the drinks stall. So many red flags. — signed a person who was dragged into doing the jeep tour

reviews.author: Oliver Taylor
reviews.text: Stunning landscape, extremely touristy though so don’t expect to get many pictures without other people in it! When we went it was VERY windy so kind of took the magic away from the place. Highly recommend bringing a bandana or face mask to protect against the sand, especially if you are on the back of an ATV. Also, like my review of the red sand dunes, unfortunately there was a lot of rubbish and litter just left on and around the sand dunes. Ruins the experience a little more!

reviews.author: Sheen Oishi
reviews.text: Absolutely gorgeous spot to catch a sunrise or sunset. You can rent a scooter and make your way in yourself or go on a tour. Tours are nothing special. So if you are confident on a scooter I’d recommend renting and save a few hundred thousand dong.

reviews.author: Kimpann
reviews.text: It's nice to see the sand dunes it's like to be in the desert. I also pay to go with the quad bike. The driver drive fast up to the top on the sand dunes and there you can take some pictures and after that we go down and some people screaming down hill. ☺️ After that you have a stop near the water. Photo time again. After that you go back to start. It was fast ride not so long time so that is little minus to it. But overall it nice visit. Minus to the price. 700 000dong for 2 people to go with the quad bikes.

reviews.author: Custin Loh
reviews.text: It’s really beautiful and worth to go. You can hire a Jeep Tour Service (around 4 hours) for around 500k VND, it can sit for 4-6 persons. Yellow color Jeep is the best color for photo shooting, you can request from them 😜. The Jeep Tour Service will bring you for photo shooting in the front of sea, visit this White Sand Dunes (you need to pay 600k VND for each ATV service, each ATV can sit 2 persons), Windmill, and also Red Sand Dunes.

reviews.author: Ân Trần
reviews.text: A good place to visit. You should go early in the morning to watch the sunrise and experience off-road vehicles. The sand is clean without trash and has a slope that is suitable for strong feelings. Off-road vehicle price for 2 people: 600 thousand VND and 800 thousand VND for self-driving.

reviews.author: Luke Halliwell
reviews.text: The white sand dunes were fab, definitely worth seeing. We didn’t go as part of a tour, and it’s 700,000 for one quad bike to go in and it’s worth every penny. It’s a short but fast ride to the top and then you can take pictures and enjoy the views and then it’s another ride around the dunes which is so fun and then you get to take pictures of the lake. Then if you want they will let you drive it back from the lake which is about 5 mins depending how fast you go! Not super budget friendly for the backpackers, but well worth it if you do want to spend a bit more than usual.

reviews.author: Cap
reviews.text: This place is a tourist trap—avoid it. There were no clear pricing instructions and no print out text on a wall &paper. At the entrance, an employee charged us 600k for an ATV ride, promising it was "unlimited." I understood this to mean multiple rides, but after one lap, they refused to let me continue. They falsely claimed "unlimited" only referred to time spent at two specific spots along the track. It felt like a complete scam. To make matters worse, the staff were rude and utterly indifferent. what they really cared is your money. I strongly do not recommend this place. 👎🏻

reviews.author: YZ P
reviews.text: We went there as part of the morning half day tour. There were a lot of tourists. You have the option of taking the ATV to and from the peak of the sand dune for ~ VND 300k, or hike up. The hike took us around 20 mins, and because it was hiking up the sand, it is a good morning workout. Most people took the ATV. But… I don’t quite see the point of taking the ATV up only to wait in the dark for the sun to rise. Coming down the sand dune was a lot easier.

reviews.author: Julien Heiligenthaler
reviews.text: Watching the sunrise from the sand dune is beautiful even though it's already quite busy in the morning. However, the presence of numerous vehicles driving through the dunes can be irritating and noisy. At the base of the dune, visitors are practically compelled to rent a quad (for 600k) to ascend. In reality, one only drives for a short distance (around 10 minutes, at most). The ascent to the top could also be done on foot, although it requires a bit more effort.

reviews.author: Elena Romanova
reviews.text: This is an interesting place. There is a fairly large area covered with sand, it was creating the feeling of being in the desert. There is a camel where you can sit and take pictures. We saw the owner beating the camel with some kind of belt. I felt sorry for the animal. You can rent a car with a driver or an ATV. A car for an hour costs 800,000 vnd, an ATV for 20 minutes costs the same. You can pay by card, no additional commissions. We were not allowed to rent a car for 30 minutes at half the price. We rented a car for an hour and were told that there would be 2 stops. The first time we stopped and could take pictures on the sand mountains. Then it started raining heavily. We were taken to the lake where we also took pictures for a while. After that we were taken to the final point. When you rent a car they tell you that you will ride for 1 hour, but in fact there are only 2 stops, and the ride itself takes a few minutes from point to point. We were upset about this because we expected to drive for an hour. Moreover, the rain prevented us from enjoying the trip. There is a cafe with reasonable price. Coffee 20 000 vnd, rice with ribs, omelette and vegetables 40 000 vnd.

reviews.author: Tristan Cambrini
reviews.text: Great opportunity to take some pictures in a deserted environment, we liked it more than the red dunes because it is bigger. However, we still thought the quads were unnecessary as it is not big enough for them anyway. If you decide to walk like we did, expect to stay at least an hour. The first ten minutes after parking are shaded, then the dunes start and it gets super hot really fast. 4 stars because they could keep it cleaner. Remember to bring water with you. Have fun!

reviews.author: Reji Doewtz
reviews.text: White Sand Dunes in Đồi cát Du lịch Hòa Thắng might be what some would call a bit of a tourist trap, and honestly, I get it. If you’re looking to drive yourself, be prepared—prices are steep, and it feels like a bit of a rip-off. The only reasonable way to go about it is by taking a jeep. That said, the sand dunes are pretty cool, and the nearby lake adds a nice touch. The little sand slides are fun for a laugh, but don’t expect any life-changing views or experiences here. It’s a nice stop if you’re in the area, but definitely manage your expectations!

reviews.author: Sam Graham
reviews.text: 5 stars for the dunes, -3 for the quad bikes = 1 star overall. Bikes were way too fast. Didn't feel safe as a walker, and walking was a whole lot safer than being on the quad bike.

reviews.author: Victor D
reviews.text: Great sand dunes. You can definitely walk instead of paying 600,000 VND for a noisy ATV ride. We started the walk at 5:11am, 15/20 minutes to walk to the top. Nice feeling to walk barefoot, not tiring. Enjoy the sunrise at 5:50ish am. Starting 8am it's way too hot to enjoy the walk. We went with a trip advisor tour guide, more of a driver
